commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
If I said That is, if I should indulge such thoughts as these. (Challoner)
I should. Hebrew, "the generation of thy children will say, that I have prevaricated. "(Pagnin)
Or, "I should offend against the "(Protestants) (Haydock)
I should not be in unison with Abraham. (St. Augustine)
I seem to declare them reprobates, and thy providence unjust. (Calmet)
It was not thus that they thought and acted, when they were under trials; (Haydock) for God chastiseth every son whom he receiveth. (Worthington)
The psalmist begins thus to enter into himself, and to correct his mistake. (Berthier)
